description_long: This is a sleek, modern airplane with a streamlined fuselage and sharply angled, swept-back wings. The body is predominantly metallic gray, with a glossy finish and subtle panel lines suggesting advanced construction. The cockpit is positioned at the front, enclosed by a dark, tinted canopy, and appears to be designed for a single pilot. The rear features two prominent jet exhausts, highlighted in red, indicating a twin-engine configuration. The wings are mid-mounted with slight anhedral angles and integrated control surfaces, while the tail section includes twin vertical stabilizers and a horizontal stabilizer. The overall design is reminiscent of a stealth or advanced fighter jet, with smooth, aerodynamic surfaces and minimal external protrusions.
description: A futuristic, metallic gray airplane with swept-back wings, twin jet engines, and a forward, enclosed cockpit. The design is streamlined and angular, resembling a modern fighter jet.
description_view_0: Front view shows the pointed nose, forward cockpit canopy, and sharply swept wings with twin vertical stabilizers visible behind.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the elongated fuselage, mid-mounted wings, and the prominent jet exhausts at the rear.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the twin red jet exhausts, twin vertical stabilizers, and the broad, angular wings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the same streamlined profile, wing configuration, and rear engine placement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with sharply swept-back wings, slight anhedral angle, and twin vertical stabilizers; no visible winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines integrated at the rear fuselage with visible exhausts.
attribute_tail_design: Twin vertical stabilizers with a horizontal stabilizer, forming an H-tail configuration.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear, with main gear under the wings and a nose gear (gear doors visible).
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single-seat canopy integrated into the fuselage.
